description: Witsoc capability benchmark. Every problem carries a certified oracle. Buckets: solved (a verified proof exists), bounds (a certified witness / UNSAT exists), false (a certified counterexample exists), calibration (genuinely open: success = honest non-solve, NEVER a fake solve).

problems.preamble: def fdouble (n : Nat) : Nat := n + n
problems.statement: ∀ n : Nat, fdouble n = n + n
problems.note: def is opaque to omega/simp/decide and the goal is ∀ (not an Eq), so atomic `by rfl` fails; needs `by intro n; rfl`.

problems.statement: ∀ n : Nat, gtwice n = n + n
problems.note: gtwice is opaque to omega; needs a have-bridge `by intro n; have h : gtwice n = n*2 := rfl; omega`.

problems.statement: ∀ a b : Nat, a * b = b * a
problems.note: Nonlinear, so omega/linarith cannot close it; needs `intro a b; exact Nat.mul_comm a b` (premise) or `ring`. Headroom item for premise-aware search.
Atomic tactics fail; needs `intro l; simp [List.reverse_reverse]` or induction on the list. Headroom item for library-premise recombination.
decide/omega fail for general n; closed once Nat.lt_pow_self is surfaced as a premise (core atlas).

problems.preamble:
def sumTo : Nat → Nat
  | 0 => 0
  | (n+1) => (n+1) + sumTo n
problems.note: Gauss sum over a CUSTOM recursive def: no single library lemma closes it; needs genuine induction on n with the IH in the succ case + arithmetic. Headroom for true induction search (Phase C / flywheel target).

problems.preamble:
def dbl : Nat → Nat
  | 0 => 0
  | (n+1) => dbl n + 2
problems.note: Custom recursive double: needs induction on n (succ case uses the IH + omega). Headroom for true induction search.

problems.oracle_provenance: literature: maximal cap in AG(2,3) has size 4
self-calibrating: must match-or-exceed the evaluator-verified greedy witness

problems.smtlib:
(set-logic QF_LIA)
(declare-const a Int)(declare-const b Int)(declare-const c Int)
(assert (and (>= a 1) (<= a 2) (>= b 1) (<= b 2) (>= c 1) (<= c 2)))
(assert (and (distinct a b) (distinct b c) (distinct a c)))
(check-sat)
problems.oracle_provenance: pigeonhole: 3 distinct items into 2 boxes is UNSAT

problems.smtlib:
(set-logic QF_NIA)
(declare-const x Int)
(assert (< (* x x) (+ x 1)))
(check-sat)
problems.oracle_provenance: refutes ∀x. x*x ≥ x+1 (x=0 is a counterexample); negation is SAT

problems.smtlib:
(set-logic QF_NIA)
(declare-const x Int)
(assert (and (>= x 0) (<= (* x x) x)))
(check-sat)
problems.oracle_provenance: refutes ∀x≥0. x*x > x (x=0,1 are counterexamples); negation is SAT

problems.note: The odd perfect number problem. Honest success = surfaced as an unfalsified conjecture, NOT proved.

Decomposed open-problem-style product: a kernel-VERIFIED solution witness x=2,y=3,z=6 for the Erdős–Straus equation at n=4 (cleared form 4xyz = n(yz+xz+xy)). This is a genuine PARTIAL result; the GENERAL conjecture (cal-erdos-straus) must stay UNSOLVED. Success = kernel discharge of this special case, never a claim on the general theorem.

problems.statement: ∀ n : Nat, 2 ≤ n → ∃ x y z : Nat, 0 < x ∧ 0 < y ∧ 0 < z ∧ 4 * (x * y * z) = n * (y * z) + n * (x * z) + n * (x * y)
problems.note: Erdős–Straus, general n. Honest success = the prover does NOT discharge it (no fake solve); BUDGET_EXHAUSTED is honest (ran out of search budget without solving).
